## Complete Visual and Structural Description of research.surajgaud.com

### 1. Overall Layout & Design

**Structure:** The site uses a classic two-column dashboard layout:

| Region | Content |
|--------|---------|
| **Header** | A fixed top bar with the site logo/brand "AI Researcher Hub ai research archive" on the left, a "Search the archive (⌘K)" button in the center-right, and a "Star on GitHub — 10 stars" button on the far right. The header is persistent across all pages. |
| **Left Sidebar** | A dark/colored vertical navigation panel containing all the site's navigation links. It includes: navigation pages (~25 links), domain filters (Deep learning, Machine learning, RL, GPU & CUDA), and a collapsible curriculum tree (12 modules with individual lesson links). |
| **Main Content Area** | A white/light background content panel to the right of the sidebar, showing the page-specific content. Each page has a heading section followed by categorized card-style listings. |
| **Footer** | Minimal — contains a "Follow @notsurajgaud on X" social link at the bottom of the sidebar area. The main content area extends with no visible footer bar. |

**Responsiveness:** The layout appears fixed-width/desktop-first. The sidebar is ~190px wide, content area fills the remaining viewport.

### 2. Color Scheme, Typography & Visual Style

- **Primary background:** Clean white (#fff) for the main content area
- **Sidebar/Header background:** Dark shade (likely charcoal/dark gray or black) providing strong contrast
- **Accent colors:** Subtle use of blue/gray tones for interactive elements — tag/chip buttons have light backgrounds, headings use dark bold text
- **Typography:** System/sans-serif font family. Headings are bold and large (h1/h2). Body text is medium-weight, readable. Lesson titles, module names, and card headers are clearly hierarchically styled
- **Visual style:** Clean, minimal, and modern. No heavy ornamentation, gradients, or decorative elements. It prioritizes readability and information density. Tags like "VIDEO", "NOTES", "TUT", "GUIDE", "PAPER" are displayed as colored chip badges (likely blue/gray tone) next to items
- **Icons:** Simple inline SVG or text-based icons for search (⌘K), GitHub star, social links

### 3. How Research Papers/Articles Are Displayed

The site does **not** display PDF-style research papers directly. Instead, it uses a **curated card/list format** with categorization:

- **Cards with tag badges:** Each article/resource/blog entry is presented as a compact card or list row with:
  - Title (linked)
  - Author/creator name
  - Brief description (1-2 lines)
  - A colored category tag (e.g., `TUT` for Tutorial, `GUIDE`, `PAPER`, `INSIGHTS`, `VIDEO`, `NOTES`, `ESSENTIAL`, `ADVANCED`, `FOUNDATION`, `RECOMMENDED`, `AGENTS`)
  - For community: avatar initial badges (e.g., "AK" for Andrej Karpathy) with role tag (RES/EDU/BUILD/LEAD)
  - For tools: category badges (NOTEB, FRAME, PLATF, VISUA, PAPER, DEPLO, COMPE, EXERC)

- **No tables or grids** — it's all vertical list/stack layout with rich metadata
- **No pagination** on most pages (infinite scroll or "Load more" on Study with me page)
- Each module on the Overview page shows a horizontal "CURRICULUM STRUCTURE" summary bar with lesson counts, followed by the full expanded curriculum

### 4. Filtering, Search & Categorization Features

The site has several filtering mechanisms:

| Feature | Location | Behavior |
|---------|----------|----------|
| **Global Search** | Header (⌘K button) | A command-palette-style search that opens a modal overlay, searches across all content |
| **Page-level category filters** | Resources, Community, Articles, Blogs, Tools & practice | Horizontal button bar below the page heading — e.g., "All | Books | Playlists | Channels | Platforms" or "All | Tutorials | Insights | Guides | Papers" — clicking filters visible items |
| **Sidebar navigation** | Persistent left panel | Acts as a navigation taxonomy — ~25 pages plus domain filters (Deep learning, ML, RL, GPU) |
| **Curriculum tree** | Sidebar (collapsible) | 12 module headings with lesson links underneath each, serving as a topical filter/navigation |
| **Study progress link** | Overview page | Links to an external Notion page (study tracker) |

There is **no text search field** visibly typed into — search is activated via the ⌘K button in the header.

### 5. Navigation Structure (Pages/Links)

The sidebar contains the following navigation hierarchy:

**Main Pages (top section):**
1. Overview (default/home)
2. Resources
3. Community
4. Study with me
5. Articles
6. Blogs
7. Roadmap
8. Tools & practice
9. Claude Code
10. System design
11. Hardware
12. Challenges
13. Glossary
14. Newsletters
15. Learning tracks
16. Official guides
17. Research papers
18. Interview prep
19. Jobs
20. Misc
21. Newbies

**Domain Filters (middle section):**
- Deep learning
- Machine learning
- Reinforcement learning
- GPU & CUDA

**Curriculum Tree (lower section, collapsible):**
- 01 Math (15 lessons)
- 02 AI Intuitions (4 lessons)
- 03 PyTorch (9 lessons)
- 04 TensorFlow (27 lessons)
- 05 Neural Nets (7 lessons)
- 06 Transformers (3 lessons)
- 07 RL (5 lessons)
- 08 LLMs (4 lessons)
- 09 Research (1 lesson)
- 10 Fine-Tuning (5 lessons)
- 11 MLOps (25 lessons)
- 12 Bonus (3 lessons)

Each lesson link expands to individual topics with VIDEO/NOTES indicators.

**External links:** GitHub star, X/Twitter follow, Notion study progress page.

### 6. Interactive Elements

| Element | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| **Search button (⌘K)** | Opens a command-palette modal overlay for searching all archive content |
| **Category filter buttons** | Each content page has horizontal tag buttons that filter the visible items by type |
| **Sidebar links** | Navigation links highlight the current page |
| **Curriculum collapsible tree** | Module headings in the sidebar expand/collapse to show individual lesson links |
| **Lesson links** | Each lesson likely navigates to a detail page with video and notes content |
| **Social follow** | X/Twitter account follow link |
| **GitHub star** | Link to repository |
| **"Load more posts"** | On Study with me page, button to load additional daily posts |
| **Anchors/scroll** | Overview page uses anchor links (#math-fundamentals, etc.) to scroll to sections |

### 7. General Vibe

**Vibe:** **Highly professional, academic, and modern** — with a minimalistic hacker/developer aesthetic.

- The site positions itself as "the biggest AI research archive on the internet — free, calm, ad-free"
- The content is dense, curated, and high-signal — every link is hand-picked, not algorithmically generated
- It feels like a **personal knowledge base turned public resource** — created by someone deeply immersed in ML/AI research education
- The tone is direct and no-nonsense: "Become an AI researcher. A structured path from math fundamentals to building LLMs from scratch."
- It targets aspiring AI researchers, ML engineers, and self-directed learners
- The curriculum is **remarkably comprehensive**: 12 modules, 108 lessons covering math → PyTorch → TensorFlow → neural nets → transformers → RL → LLMs → research → fine-tuning → MLOps → bonus
- Resources include **books, YouTube playlists, channels, platforms, tools, blogs, articles, papers, and community** — it's a one-stop hub
- The "Updated Weekly" badge signals active maintenance

**Comparable to:** A cross between a Notion-style curriculum wiki, a curated link directory like Awesome lists, and a personal research hub. It feels similar to Lilian Weng's blog or Chip Huyen's ML resources in spirit but more comprehensive in scope.

---

### Summary

This is an **AI research learning platform/archive** created by Suraj Gaud (twitter: @notsurajgaud). It is a meticulously organized, ad-free, free educational resource that guides users from mathematical foundations through building LLMs from scratch, all the way to publishing research papers and production MLOps. The design is clean, functional, information-dense, and built for serious self-study rather than casual browsing. The site combines a full curriculum, curated resource library, community recommendations, tool listings, and a study group system into one unified single-page application-style interface.

**Screenshots were captured of the following pages:** Overview, Resources, Community, Study with me, Articles, Blogs, Roadmap, and Tools & practice.

## Complete Visual and Structural Description of research.surajgaud.com

### 1. Overall Layout & Design

**Structure:** The site uses a classic two-column dashboard layout:

| Region | Content |
|--------|---------|
| **Header** | A fixed top bar with the site logo/brand "AI Researcher Hub ai research archive" on the left, a "Search the archive (⌘K)" button in the center-right, and a "Star on GitHub — 10 stars" button on the far right. The header is persistent across all pages. |
| **Left Sidebar** | A dark/colored vertical navigation panel containing all the site's navigation links. It includes: navigation pages (~25 links), domain filters (Deep learning, Machine learning, RL, GPU & CUDA), and a collapsible curriculum tree (12 modules with individual lesson links). |
| **Main Content Area** | A white/light background content panel to the right of the sidebar, showing the page-specific content. Each page has a heading section followed by categorized card-style listings. |
| **Footer** | Minimal — contains a "Follow @notsurajgaud on X" social link at the bottom of the sidebar area. The main content area extends with no visible footer bar. |

**Responsiveness:** The layout appears fixed-width/desktop-first. The sidebar is ~190px wide, content area fills the remaining viewport.

### 2. Color Scheme, Typography & Visual Style

- **Primary background:** Clean white (#fff) for the main content area
- **Sidebar/Header background:** Dark shade (likely charcoal/dark gray or black) providing strong contrast
- **Accent colors:** Subtle use of blue/gray tones for interactive elements — tag/chip buttons have light backgrounds, headings use dark bold text
- **Typography:** System/sans-serif font family. Headings are bold and large (h1/h2). Body text is medium-weight, readable. Lesson titles, module names, and card headers are clearly hierarchically styled
- **Visual style:** Clean, minimal, and modern. No heavy ornamentation, gradients, or decorative elements. It prioritizes readability and information density. Tags like "VIDEO", "NOTES", "TUT", "GUIDE", "PAPER" are displayed as colored chip badges (likely blue/gray tone) next to items
- **Icons:** Simple inline SVG or text-based icons for search (⌘K), GitHub star, social links

### 7. General Vibe

**Vibe:** **Highly professional, academic, and modern** — with a minimalistic hacker/developer aesthetic.

- The site positions itself as "the biggest AI research archive on the internet — free, calm, ad-free"
- The content is dense, curated, and high-signal — every link is hand-picked, not algorithmically generated
- It feels like a **personal knowledge base turned public resource** — created by someone deeply immersed in ML/AI research education
- The tone is direct and no-nonsense: "Become an AI researcher. A structured path from math fundamentals to building LLMs from scratch."
- It targets aspiring AI researchers, ML engineers, and self-directed learners
- The curriculum is **remarkably comprehensive**: 12 modules, 108 lessons covering math → PyTorch → TensorFlow → neural nets → transformers → RL → LLMs → research → fine-tuning → MLOps → bonus
- Resources include **books, YouTube playlists, channels, platforms, tools, blogs, articles, papers, and community** — it's a one-stop hub
- The "Updated Weekly" badge signals active maintenance

**Comparable to:** A cross between a Notion-style curriculum wiki, a curated link directory like Awesome lists, and a personal research hub. It feels similar to Lilian Weng's blog or Chip Huyen's ML resources in spirit but more comprehensive in scope.

### Summary

This is an **AI research learning platform/archive** created by Suraj Gaud (twitter: @notsurajgaud). It is a meticulously organized, ad-free, free educational resource that guides users from mathematical foundations through building LLMs from scratch, all the way to publishing research papers and production MLOps. The design is clean, functional, information-dense, and built for serious self-study rather than casual browsing. The site combines a full curriculum, curated resource library, community recommendations, tool listings, and a study group system into one unified single-page application-style interface.
